FAM110A.name: family with sequence similarity 110 member A
FAM110A.summary_50w: Predicted to be located in cytoplasm; microtubule organizing center; and spindle pole. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM110B.name: family with sequence similarity 110 member B
FAM110B.summary_50w: Located in cytosol and mitochondrion. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM111B.name: FAM111 trypsin like peptidase B
FAM111B.summary_50w: This gene encodes a protein with a trypsin-like cysteine/serine peptidase domain in the C-terminus. Mutations in this gene are associated with an autosomal dominant form of hereditary fibrosing poikiloderma (HFP). Affected individuals display mottled pigmentation, telangiectasia, epidermal atrophy, tendon contractures, and progressive pulmonary fibrosis. Alternative splicing results in multiple transcript...

FAM120A.name: family with sequence similarity 120 member A

FAM120AOS.name: family with sequence similarity 120 member A opposite strand
FAM120AOS.summary_50w: Differences in the expression level of this gene are associated with the survival rate of those with glioma. [provided by RefSeq, May 2017].

FAM120C.name: family with sequence similarity 120 member C
FAM120C.summary_50w: This gene encodes a potential transmembrane protein and lies in a region where mutations and deletions have been associated with intellectual disability and autism. Alternative splicing results in multiple transcript variants. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2011].

FAM131C.name: family with sequence similarity 131 member C

FAM135A.name: family with sequence similarity 135 member A

FAM13A.name: family with sequence similarity 13 member A

FAM13B.name: family with sequence similarity 13 member B
FAM13B.summary_50w: Predicted to enable GTPase activator activity. Predicted to be involved in regulation of small GTPase mediated signal transduction. Predicted to be located in cytosol. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM161B.name: FAM161 centrosomal protein B
FAM161B.summary_50w: Predicted to be involved in cilium organization. Located in cytoplasmic microtubule. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM162B.name: family with sequence similarity 162 member B

FAM174B.name: family with sequence similarity 174 member B
FAM174B.summary_50w: Involved in Golgi organization. Located in Golgi apparatus and plasma membrane. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM180B.name: family with sequence similarity 180 member B
FAM180B.summary_50w: Predicted to be located in extracellular region. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM20A.name: FAM20A golgi associated secretory pathway pseudokinase
FAM20A.summary_50w: This locus encodes a protein that is likely secreted and may function in hematopoiesis. A mutation at this locus has been associated with amelogenesis imperfecta and gingival hyperplasia syndrome. Alternatively spliced transcript variants have been identified. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2011].

FAM20C.name: FAM20C golgi associated secretory pathway kinase
FAM20C.summary_50w: This gene encodes a member of the family of secreted protein kinases. The encoded protein binds calcium and phosphorylates proteins involved in bone mineralization. Mutations in this gene are associated with the autosomal recessive disorder Raine syndrome. [provided by RefSeq, Apr 2014].

FAM3D.name: FAM3 metabolism regulating signaling molecule D
FAM3D.summary_50w: Involved in negative regulation of insulin secretion. Predicted to be located in extracellular region. Predicted to be integral component of membrane. Predicted to be active in extracellular space. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM47E.name: family with sequence similarity 47 member E
FAM47E.summary_50w: Enables enzyme activator activity. Involved in positive regulation of histone methylation and protein localization to chromatin. Located in chromatin; cytoplasm; and nucleus. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM53A.name: family with sequence similarity 53 member A
FAM53A.summary_50w: Predicted to be involved in protein import into nucleus. Predicted to be active in nucleus. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM72A.name: family with sequence similarity 72 member A
FAM72A.summary_50w: Predicted to act upstream of or within negative regulation of brain-derived neurotrophic factor-activated receptor activity and positive regulation of apoptotic process. FAM72B.name: family with sequence similarity 72 member B
FAM72B.summary_50w: Located in cytosol and intracellular membrane-bounded organelle. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM72D.name: family with sequence similarity 72 member D
FAM72D.summary_50w: Located in cytosol and intracellular membrane-bounded organelle. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM81B.name: family with sequence similarity 81 member B
FAM81B.summary_50w: Located in nucleus. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM86B1.name: family with sequence similarity 86 member B1 (gene/pseudogene)
FAM86B1.summary_50w: Predicted to enable methyltransferase activity. Predicted to be involved in methylation. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM90A1.name: family with sequence similarity 90 member A1
FAM90A1.summary_50w: FAM90A1 belongs to subfamily I of the primate-specific FAM90A gene family, which originated from multiple duplications and rearrangements (Bosch et al., 2007 [PubMed 17684299]).[supplied by OMIM, Oct 2009].

FAM95B1.name: family with sequence similarity 95 member B1

FAM98A.summary_50w: Enables protein methyltransferase activity. Involved in positive regulation of cell population proliferation; positive regulation of gene expression; and protein methylation. Predicted to be part of tRNA-splicing ligase complex. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Apr 2022]

FAM9A.name: family with sequence similarity 9 member A
FAM9A.summary_50w: This gene is a member of a gene family which arose through duplication on the X chromosome. The encoded protein may be a nuclear protein that is localized to the nucleolus, and has some similarity to a synaptonemal complex protein. Multiple alternatively spliced variants, encoding the same protein, have been...

FANCA.name: FA complementation group A
FANCA.summary_50w: The Fanconi anemia complementation group (FANC) currently includes FANCA, FANCB, FANCC, FANCD1 (also called BRCA2), FANCD2, FANCE, FANCF, FANCG, FANCI, FANCJ (also called BRIP1), FANCL, FANCM and FANCN (also called PALB2).
